title: How does the entry plug land after ejection
display_title: How does the entry plug land after ejection
wikitext:
[]: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by mys_721tx » Wed Dec 20, 2017 8:12 pm In Eva, whether the entry plug ejection system functions or not is largely determined by the plot. In NGE, the entry plug has been ejected three times, two of which are "on camera". Rei ejected from Unit-00 during the activation test. (Ep. 05) Toji partially ejected from Unit-03. (Ep. 18) Asuka ejected off camera after Unit-02 was attacked by Unit-03. (Ep. 18) In RoE, especially in 3.33, the success rate rose dramatically: Rei ejected from Unit-00 during the activation test. (1.11) Asuka partially ejected from Unit-03. (2.22) Rei ejected from Mark. 09 after SEELE's program took over. (3.33) Asuka ejected from Unit-02 a bit later. (3.33) Shinji was forcibly ejected from Unit-13 by Mari. (3.33) I guess they did learn a thing or two about ejection system in fourteen years. However, nether the TV series or the Rebuild movies have never shown how the entry plug should land when it is successfully ejected. Considering the injury Rei suffered and Gendo's reaction when the ejection system was engaged, her experience probably was not the norm. In 3.33, we saw Shinji's plug plowed into the ground. The trio were ambulatory and none of them had visible injuries. It is probably safe to assume that the system worked as intended. The landing site might give us some clue about the landing process: There was no sign of parachutes or air bags on the ground. The scene is not particularly windy; suppose parachutes were deployed and cut after landing, they would not drift too far. The nozzle on the top of the plug are charred. However, since those are the nozzle for the ejection rockets, it is impossible to tell if they had been fired again before landing. Do the plug cushion the fall at all?

[]: Re: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by Hark » Wed Dec 20, 2017 8:51 pm given what the plug needs to be able to withstand while in a eva i believe its safe to assume that the plug can withstand a drop after being propelled from the eva itself into the ground . leaving the pilot with hopefully no more injury than what they have already from being in the eva, but as with all things there are exceptions. since the plug is filled with lcl fluid i would think that that extra buffer in between a harsh drop would hopefully have a fair bit of cushioning , since the pilots are not exactly "strapped in" to any safety harness , since you can see plenty of times where Asuka or Shinji moving around the cockpit with no hindrance by any straps. upon ejection and landing of the plug lcl fluid appears to have been drained upon landing and before it is opened by gendo while saving Rei. i do think its a safe bet to assume to a degree that the plugs may have a minor set of thrusters around the pod relative to the angle and direction of impact on either end or on one end of the pod to help with slowing its velocity. you can see something similar to this happen when one of the evas eject there umbicle , and it falls to the ground , but not before activating its thrusters for a brief amount of time close to the ground, so it can make a "soft" landing as to not cause further damage we know that the ejection system has relatively powerful rockets do to its size , and the amount of heat that is left on the outer hull of the plug "as seen when gendo is opening the plug for Rei" as far as gendos reaction to Rei ejection , i feel its warranted, just as in real life with jets . just because there is a ejector seat for saving the piloits life does not mean something else might not happen causing failure , its dangerous in its own merits. mishaps happen all the time and as far as escape methods go there designed to save lives but are not always reliable , given what there installed into and what the thing its being installed into needs to do on the daily and all the stress that comes with it ,its scary thinking that this last ditch thing is between you living and dying.

[]: Re: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by mys_721tx » Wed Dec 20, 2017 9:37 pm Hark wrote: you can see something similar to this happen when one of the evas eject there umbicle , and it falls to the ground , but not before activating its thrusters for a brief amount of time close to the ground, so it can make a "soft" landing as to not cause further damage

[]: Re: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by Hark » Wed Dec 20, 2017 11:31 pm well allas for me i cannot comment on the rebuild plugs , as i have not seen the rebuild yet , not that i dont want to but i like having the right conditions to watch eva specifically, i like to give it my undivided attention. that and im playing through some of the games before i watch the rebuild anyways :3. but as far as the LCL goes , yes it seems i mis-remebered what happened Perhaps its just Landing engines paired with the LCL as an extra cushion to absorb the impact almost like how you can suspend something in water and it becomes significantly harder to move around in the liquid if there is no air pockets to cause turbulence. (almost like how you can tell the difference between a hard boiled egg and raw egg. the hard boiled one if you spin it spins fast . where as a raw egg if you spin it , spins very slow in comparison , that is because there is liquid on the inside and it is much less susceptible to change in momentum than if it was one solid piece like the hard boiled egg ) but i figure that LCL is perhaps less dense than water because you can see the pilots moving at around the same speed as they would outside of the plug, since in compassion to water moving just as fast in the air is much slower in water if not takes much more energy to do the same amount of work. but regardless of LCL and how dense it is and what not , i figure that the insides of the plug could be made with a high impact absorbing/ dampening material , perhaps akin to some dense foam , perhaps with some crumple zones. and i would say that in comparison to its size to the evas and since the evas can break the plugs like toothpicks , the plugs themselves are actually quite sturdy "when not being crushed by a eva anyways" the plugs hull seems to be pretty strong , and i would say at least 5 to 1/2 feet thick at least with what the pictures i have been seeing.

[]: Re: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Dec 21, 2017 1:53 pm There is a parachute at the end of Q, it's the olive drab colored cloth on the right side of the screen The entry plug landing system has been designed back during the original TV series and it's pretty much in every book that has the mechanical settei. The concept art is there, it just wasn't implements fully. The spiral rocket motors they did put in the show because that looks cool as hell but after that it basically follows the same as the Launch Escape System where the used up rocket motors are jettisoned to reduce weight and a main chute is deployed by a pilot chute, with some extra retro rockets the moment before landing. I can understand them not fully implementing this in Q either because despite this would have been great technological fetishism that Anno and Co. are known for, at that point in the movie it's simply not the time for it. The emotional aspects are the focus and thus you keep the entry plug intact, familiar to the audience so it doesn't become a distraction.

[]: Re: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Dec 21, 2017 3:03 pm The pilots breathe in the LCL, that should provide them a lot of shock protection.

[]: Re: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by mys_721tx » Thu Dec 21, 2017 9:42 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: There is a parachute at the end of Q, it's the olive drab colored cloth on the right side of the screen

[]: Re: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by Joseki » Fri Dec 22, 2017 7:45 am Chuckman wrote: The pilots breathe in the LCL, that should provide them a lot of shock protection.

[]: Re: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by Cybermat47 » Fri Dec 22, 2017 8:17 am Chuckman wrote: The pilots breathe in the LCL, that should provide them a lot of shock protection.

[]: Re: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by Ray » Sun Dec 31, 2017 12:49 am Cybermat47 wrote: Not enough, judging from Rei’s condition in the aftermath of the 00 berserk incident. She could barely even stand on the night of Sachiel’s assault on NERV. Things could be different in Rebuild, though.

[]: Re: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Dec 31, 2017 4:27 am Ray wrote: I assumed that was from the Angel cooking the LCL inside the pod.

[]: Re: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Sun Dec 31, 2017 12:36 pm Sachi wrote: The exterior burned Gendo's hands, but Rei was not experiencing that heat within the plug. Her injuries look more like she was battered and bruised, which fits with having fallen from the height she did in the contact experiment. Even if the entry plug had ejected a parachute, the room simply wasn't big enough for it to be at all useful. The LCL probably absorbed some of the shock, but not enough to prevent her injuries.

[]: Re: How does the entry plug land after ejection?             Quote Post by Cosmo11 » Sun Dec 31, 2017 11:14 pm ^ I don't think it would be that complicated. During the test, it was grinding along the wall of the test room at high speed while its ejection rockets were still going off. That is going to produce a huge amount of heat. Ramiel's beam was also shown to visibly melt the Eva's armour and boil the LCL within the plug.
